mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing earnings downgrades and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, negating the supply constraint mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation print exceeding 4.0% forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy, causing immediate equity drawdowns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms labor market resilience, sustaining consumer spending expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE change is within +/- 0.1pp
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate revenue streams, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below the 0.5% threshold during recovery phases.
